# QARoom

> A multi-tenant social platform built to demonstrate testing-driven architecture.
> Microservices monorepo; TypeScript end-to-end. This is the agent-discovery entry point.

QARoom is built across 10 milestones. The current milestone determines which services exist
(see `/AGENTS.md` → "Milestone awareness"). Every service is contract-first (Zod → OpenAPI),
deterministic (injected Clock/IdGenerator/Randomness), and observable (`/system/state`
and `/system/capabilities` per service). Every error is RFC 7807 `application/problem+json`.

## Start here
- Repo conventions and how to make changes: /AGENTS.md (symlinked as /CLAUDE.md)
- Architecture, strategy, roadmap, conventions, ADRs: /docs (read in numbered order)
- Immutable foundational decisions: /docs/adr/0001-foundational-decisions.md
- Service-level SLOs: /docs/slos.md

## Services (Milestone 0)
- content-service — posts and votes within communities.
  - Contract: /services/content/openapi.yaml
  - Agent reference: /services/content/AGENTS.md
  - Discovery: /services/content/.well-known/llms.txt
  - Live capabilities: GET /system/capabilities (MCP-tool-shaped)

## Conventions an agent must respect
- Schemas are the source of truth (`packages/contracts`); never hand-edit generated OpenAPI.
- Mutations carry `Idempotency-Key`; every mutating endpoint declares OAS `links`.
- Determinism is enforced by lint: no `new Date()` / `Math.random()` / unseeded UUID in runtime code.
- Test outputs are machine-readable: `test-results/summary.json` (frozen schema).
